skill.name: otel-expert
skill.description: Use this skill for OpenTelemetry guidance, including semantic conventions, collector configuration, OTLP, instrumentation, traces, metrics, and logs.
skill.summary: Answer OpenTelemetry questions using bundled documentation and source-backed guidance.
skill.author: ANcpLua

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks, documentation paths, and possible weak cryptography keywords in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives: the skill only instructs the assistant to read bundled OpenTelemetry docs and use allowed Read, Grep, and Glob tools. No prompt injection, network access, executable script, credential access, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The six external command findings are markdown inline code and a fenced example showing Grep tool usage against bundled documentation paths. The skill declares only Read, Grep, and Glob as allowed tools and does not request shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain documentation paths and Grep examples, not executable shell commands. The allowed tools list excludes shell access, which strongly supports a false positive verdict.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings at the cited lines do not reference any cryptographic algorithm or implementation. Line 3 is the skill description, and line 51 describes an attributes table in the response format.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review of the cited lines found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithm usage. The scanner appears to have matched benign text or formatting context.

content.value_statement: OpenTelemetry work often requires precise semantic convention and collector details. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to search bundled OTel documentation and answer with source references.
actual_capabilities: Searches bundled OpenTelemetry documentation for semantic conventions and attributes.
Guides collector configuration questions for receivers, processors, and exporters.
Supports OTLP protocol and telemetry pipeline explanations.
Prioritizes .NET instrumentation patterns when examples are requested.
Encourages answers with direct source references from the documentation set.
limitations: Depends on the bundled documentation being present and current.
Does not include vendor-specific exporter recommendations by default.
Does not run collectors, validate live telemetry, or inspect production systems.
Focuses on stable semantic conventions and avoids deprecated attributes.

use_cases.title: Choose Semantic Attributes
use_cases.description: A backend engineer can identify correct OpenTelemetry attributes for spans, metrics, and logs.

use_cases.title: Plan Collector Configuration
use_cases.description: A platform engineer can draft collector pipeline guidance for OTLP receivers, processors, and exporters.

use_cases.title: Review Instrumentation Guidance
use_cases.description: An observability lead can check whether instrumentation advice follows stable conventions and source documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Find Attributes
prompt_templates.prompt: Which OpenTelemetry attributes should I use for this span: [describe operation]? Include attribute names and source references.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Collector Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how to configure an OpenTelemetry Collector pipeline for [source] to OTLP export. Mention receivers, processors, and exporters.

prompt_templates.title: Review Instrumentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this instrumentation plan for OpenTelemetry correctness: [paste plan]. Flag deprecated attributes and suggest stable alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Design Telemetry Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an OpenTelemetry telemetry model for [service or workflow]. Include traces, metrics, logs, attributes, and collector considerations.

output_examples.input: What attributes should I use for an HTTP client span?
output_examples.output: A concise attribute table with stable HTTP semantic convention fields, usage notes, and the documentation file used as the source.

output_examples.input: How should I configure the collector for OTLP input and export?
output_examples.output: A pipeline explanation covering receiver, processor, and exporter roles, with warnings from the bundled sync report when present.

output_examples.input: Review my .NET telemetry plan for a background worker.
output_examples.output: A structured review of traces, metrics, and logs, with .NET-oriented instrumentation guidance and source references.

best_practices: Ask for the telemetry signal, runtime, and target operation before choosing attributes.
Check the index and sync report before answering version-sensitive questions.
Include source references so users can verify the guidance.
anti_patterns: Do not invent semantic convention attributes when documentation is missing.
Do not recommend deprecated attributes unless explaining a migration path.
Do not assume vendor-specific exporter behavior for general OTel questions.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps answer OpenTelemetry questions about semantic conventions, collector configuration, OTLP, and instrumentation.

faq.question: Does it use external documentation?
faq.answer: It is designed to use bundled documentation under the plugin root when that documentation is available.

faq.question: Can it generate collector configuration?
faq.answer: It can explain collector configuration structure and suggest pipeline components based on the bundled docs.

faq.question: Does it support vendor-specific exporters?
faq.answer: No. The skill assumes OTLP export and avoids vendor-specific exporter guidance by default.

faq.question: Can it validate live telemetry data?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation-backed guidance but does not inspect running systems or telemetry backends.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
